data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good picture of the project. Let me summarize the time-related static calls and other hard-to-test statics. The user specifically asked about time-related static calls, but the project has many other statics too. Let me focus on what they asked about but also mention the other statics.

Time-related static calls:
1. `OrderProcessor.cs:21` — `DateTime.UtcNow` (CreatedAt assignment)
2. `OrderProcessor.cs:22` — `DateTime.UtcNow.AddDays(30)` (ExpiresAt assignment)
3. 
`OrderProcessor.cs:25` — `DateTime.Now` (receipt string)
4. `OrderProcessor.cs:34` — `DateTime.UtcNow` (IsExpired check)
5. `NotificationService.cs:7` — `DateTime.UtcNow` (daysUntilExpiry calc)
6. `NotificationService.cs:16` — `DateTime.UtcNow` (log file name)
7. `NotificationService.cs:18` — `DateTime.UtcNow` (log entry timestamp)

So I've found seven instances of DateTime calls scattered across OrderProcessor and NotificationService. Since this is a .NET project, I should consider how to abstract these for testability.
